---
title: "Figure 4 (distortion) — the second moment across the design space: LSE, NR, R, IR"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
THE SECOND-MOMENT HALF of the old Figure 4, split in two (Luciano, 2026-07-23) because four
algorithms carrying both moments would run to sixteen map rows. This half: the ellipses plus the
INFORMATION DISTORTION, from battery_pool_G anchored at theta_pool. The first-moment half is
figure_4_bias.Rmd, which carries the same ellipse block.

BLOCK A, the ellipses, is the referent: a distortion map is a colour field over a ratio of a score
covariance to a Fisher information, and nobody has an intuition for 1.44. The cloud says it in the
form a reader already knows — here is how your estimates scatter, here is what the algorithm said
they would (dashed), here is what the sandwich correction says they would (dotted).

CAUTION the panel prints and the caption must repeat: THE ELLIPSE AND THE MAP ARE DIFFERENT OBJECTS.
The ellipse compares Cov(theta_hat) against the sandwich MARGINALISED over the other parameters; the
maps compare score against Fisher unmarginalised. The marginal reading is systematically milder, so
the two numbers printed per panel (ellipse ratio and the map's own value at the same cell) are
expected to disagree, and the disagreement is stated rather than hidden.

PLANE: one panel per N_ch (columns), x = Delta*k_off, y = raw instrumental noise. ROWS are parameter
(outer) then algorithm (inner), so the four rungs of a parameter are adjacent.

WHICH DIRECTION: the primary render is k[off], the direction that carries the distortion (R's k_off
distortion is a flat floor near 1.4 across four decades while IR converges to 1). The both-directions
variant is also written: the N_ch direction crosses from conservative to over-confident, the OPPOSITE
sign, which is why any scalar averaged over parameters cancels them and reports a faithful algorithm.

All machinery, traps and scales live in figure_4_common.R, shared with the bias half.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

```{r common, include=FALSE}
source("figure_4_common.R")
```

```{r ellipses, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 9.5}
gA <- build_gA()
gA
```

```{r assemble}
# one assembler, the parameter roster is the argument. Block A is shared and unchanged: an ellipse
# needs two axes, so it stays on the (k_off, N_ch) pair whatever the maps show.
assemble_dist <- function(params) {
  hA <- 2.30 * max(1, length(ELL_ALGOS))
  hC <- map_height(length(params), 0.475)         # 0.95 in per 2-decade row (carries the x axis)
  g <- btitle("A   grouped MLE distributions") /
       gA /
       btitle("B   information distortion") /
       mapblock(stat, function(d) log10(d$Dconf), DIST_PAL, DIST_BREAKS, params,
                top = TRUE, bottom = TRUE) /
       colorbar(DIST_PAL, DIST_LAB, "distortion", DIST_LIN, sol = c(0.87, 1.15), dsh = c(0.67, 1.5))
  hs <- c(0.16, hA, 0.16, hC, 0.28)
  list(g = g + plot_layout(heights = hs), h = sum(hs))
}
```

```{r render-primary, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 12}
# PRIMARY: k[off] alone, the direction that carries the distortion. Four map rows, one per algorithm.
prim <- assemble_dist("off")
prim$g
ggsave("Figure_4_distortion.pdf", prim$g, width = 7.0, height = prim$h)
cat("Figure_4_distortion.pdf: distortion on k_off; height", round(prim$h, 2), "in\n")
```

```{r render-both-params, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 18}
# VARIANT: both directions. The N_ch direction carries the OPPOSITE sign to k_off, so a scalar
# averaged over parameters cancels them and reports a faithful algorithm. Only this render makes
# that verifiable, and it is the one that shows the two moments are not in the same direction.
both <- assemble_dist(PARAM_LONG)
both$g
ggsave("Figure_4_distortion_both_params.pdf", both$g, width = 7.0, height = both$h)
cat("Figure_4_distortion_both_params.pdf: k_off and N_ch; height", round(both$h, 2), "in\n")
```

```{r caption-numbers}
cat("\n=== Figure 4 (distortion) caption numbers ===\n")
cat("\nDISTORTION, median over the 7 intervals, by algorithm / parameter / N_ch (noise 0.1)\n")
print(as.data.frame(stat %>% filter(comp == "total", noise == "0.1") %>%
        group_by(param, algo, Num_ch) %>% summarise(med = median(Dconf), .groups = "drop") %>%
        mutate(algo = ALGO_LAB[algo]) %>%
        pivot_wider(names_from = Num_ch, values_from = med)), digits = 3, row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nDOES NOISE CURE IT? same medians at each noise level, k_off\n")
print(as.data.frame(stat %>% filter(comp == "total", param == "off") %>%
        group_by(algo, noise, Num_ch) %>% summarise(med = median(Dconf), .groups = "drop") %>%
        mutate(algo = ALGO_LAB[algo]) %>%
        pivot_wider(names_from = Num_ch, values_from = med) %>%
        arrange(algo, as.numeric(noise))), digits = 3, row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nEXTENT: fraction of (interval, noise) cells with |D-1| >= 0.15\n")
print(as.data.frame(stat %>% filter(comp == "total") %>% group_by(param, algo, Num_ch) %>%
        summarise(f = mean(abs(Dconf - 1) >= 0.15), .groups = "drop") %>%
        mutate(algo = ALGO_LAB[algo]) %>%
        pivot_wider(names_from = Num_ch, values_from = f)), digits = 2, row.names = FALSE)
```
